original_abstract:
This study investigates the formation of secondary organic aerosol (SOA) from the oxidation of $\alpha$-pinene, comparing ozonolysis and hydroxyl radical (OH)-initiated pathways. Experiments were conducted in the University of North Carolina’s dual outdoor smog chamber facility under high nitric oxide (NO) conditions, with varying relative humidity (RH) and aerosol acidity. We found that ozonolysis produced significantly more SOA mass than OH-initiated oxidation under initially high-NO conditions, though RH had no discernible impact on the total SOA mass. Interestingly, when $\alpha$-pinene was oxidized by ozone in the presence of seed aerosol already coated with OH-derived organic matter, new particle nucleation was suppressed compared to experiments using pure ammonium sulfate seeds.

To understand these trends, we characterized the chemical composition of the SOA using UPLC/ESI-HR-Q-TOFMS, specifically targeting carboxylic acids and high-molecular-weight dimers. While eight carboxylic acids and four dimers were identified—accounting for 8–12% of the total SOA mass—their distribution varied strictly by oxidant. OH-initiated oxidation favored highly oxidized monomeric species like 3-methyl-1,2,3-butanetricarboxylic acid (MBTCA) and diaterpenylic acid acetate (DTAA). In contrast, dimers were only detected in SOA formed via ozonolysis. These dimers were twice as abundant at high RH (50–90%) compared to low RHed conditions (30–50%), a trend that correlates with increased new particle formation. Notably, aerosol acidity had a negligible effect on dimer concentrations.

Our results suggest that these dimers do not form through traditional particle-phase esterification, as previously thought. Instead, the data support a gas-phase mechanism involving stabilized Criegee Intermediates (sCI) produced during ozonolysis. The low volatility and high polarity of these gas-phase dimers likely drive homogeneous nucleation, increasing particle number concentrations. Given that three of these dimers (MW 358, 344, and 368) are frequently detected in ambient forest aerosols, we propose they serve as reliable molecular tracers for $\alpha$-pinene ozonolysis. Their presence in the atmosphere is a key indicator of ozone-driven aging processes and, by extension, the influence of anthropogenic pollution on biogenic aerosol formation.
humanized_abstract:
Secondary organic aerosol (SOA) formation from the ozonolysis and hydroxyl radical (OH) reactions of alpha-pinene (α-pinene) in a dual outdoor smog chamber The purpose of this study was to investigate secondary organic aerosol (SOA) formation from the oxidation of α-pinene by comparing the amounts of SOA formed by ozonolysis and OH reactions. These experiments were carried out in the University of North Carolina (UNC)’s dual outdoor smog chamber facility under high nitric oxide (NO) conditions at different levels of relative humidity (RH) and aerosol acidity. The results indicated that the ozonolysis formed more SOA mass than the OH reactions under the initially high-NO conditions, regardless of RH level. Furthermore, when α-pinene was oxidized by ozone in the presence of aerosol seed having a coating of OH-derived organic matter, the particle nucleation was suppressed in comparison with pure ammonium sulfate seed aerosol.

Details of the trends seen are reported and includes UPLC/ESI-HR-Q-TOFMS analysis of the resulting SOA chemistry, focusing on carboxylic acids and HMW dimers. Eight carboxylic acids and four dimers were identified accounting for 8–12% of the mass of the SOA and were produced strictly in response to the oxidant. Oh radicals favoured the production of highly oxidised monomers such as 3-methyl-1,2,3-butanetricarboxylic acid (MBTCA), diaterpenylic acid acetate (DTAA) while dimers were only seen in the ozonolysis pathways. These were higher at high RH levels (50–90%) than low RH levels (30–50%) which correlates with the NPOTs seen under these conditions. The level of aerosol acidity has a negligible impact on dimer yields.

Our measurements indicate that these dimers are not formed via the traditional particle-phase esterification route previously proposed. Rather, our data suggest that they are formed in the gas phase following the stabilization of Criegee intermediates (sCI) produced during ozonolysis. Their low volatility and high polarity are thought to promote homogeneous nucleation and, as a result, enhance particle number concentrations. As three of the dimers (MW 358, 344 and 368 Da) are consistently measured in ambient forest aerosols, we propose that they are good molecular tracers of $\alpha$-pinene ozonolysis occurring in the atmosphere. As such, their atmospheric presence is a useful indicator of the ozone-driven aerosol aging processes that are affected by anthropogenic pollution.

document.output:

document.input:
This study investigates the formation of secondary organic aerosol (SOA) from the oxidation of $\alpha$-pinene, comparing ozonolysis and hydroxyl radical (OH)-initiated pathways. Experiments were conducted in the University of North Carolina’s dual outdoor smog chamber facility under high nitric oxide (NO) conditions, with varying relative humidity (RH) and aerosol acidity. We found that ozonolysis produced significantly more SOA mass than OH-initiated oxidation under initially high-NO conditions, though RH had no discernible impact on the total SOA mass. In contrast, dimers were only detected in SOA formed via ozonolysis. Instead, the data support a gas-phase mechanism involving stabilized Criegee Intermediates (sCI) produced during ozonolysis. The low volatility and high polarity of these gas-phase dimers likely drive homogeneous nucleation, increasing particle number concentrations. Given that three of these dimers (MW 358, 344, and 368) are frequently detected in ambient forest aerosols, we propose they serve as reliable molecular tracers for $\alpha$-pinene ozonolysis. Their presence in the atmosphere is a key indicator of ozone-driven aging processes and, by extension, the influence of anthropogenic pollution on biogenic aerosol formation.
